steps.title: Convert the radical to exponential form
steps.content: The expression $\sqrt[a]{r^{48}}$ can be rewritten as $r^{48/a}$ using the exponent rule: $\sqrt[n]{x^m} = x^{m/n}$. This converts the radical notation to an exponential form we can work with.

steps.title: Set the exponents equal
steps.content: Since $r^{48/a}$ must equal $r^{8/9}$ and $r \gt 1$, the exponents must be equal: $$\frac{48}{a} = \frac{8}{9}$$

steps.title: Solve for a
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $a$ and by $9$: $$48 \cdot 9 = 8a$$ $$432 = 8a$$ $$a = \frac{432}{8} = 54$$
